name: ElectroBOOM
oneLine: Iranian-Canadian electrical engineer who teaches electronics through slapstick and intentionally-staged shocks, sparks, and small explosions.
bio: Mehdi Sadaghdar holds an electronics degree from the University of Tehran and a microelectronics master's from Simon Fraser University. ElectroBOOM mixes earnest electrical engineering tutorials with comedic mishaps where he reacts to shocks, fires, and exploding components for laughs while showing what not to do. Series include the beginner-focused ElectroBOOM101 and a steady stream of debunks of viral 'free energy' and pseudoscience videos.
style: comedic on-camera electronics demos with slapstick reactions, whiteboard explanations, and bench-lab footage
notable: debunks of 'free energy' and overunity claims
collaborations with major science creators on electricity topics
